verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-related peptidase 2) is a serine protease synthesized as a secreted protein with a canonical signal peptide. It is constitutively secreted into blood plasma and tissue fluids with no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. While KLK2 is a biomarker in some cancers and is detected in serum, it remains in equilibrium with the soluble pool and is not stably anchored to any cell-surface transmembrane partner via covalent or wash-resistant non-covalent interaction. No documented cell-state-induced surface display (cancer ecto-pool, cell-death externalization, or activation-induced translocation) exists. No tissue-restricted surface pool, no lysosomal exocytosis mechanism, no documented PM minority residence. The canonical biology is constitutive secretion; the protein body does not reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane via its own mechanism or stable partnership.
